A_methods:
for teaching agriculture.
Of the four universities participating Cornell is the only institution training teachers of agriculture. As the project enters its planning year, the Cornell
Agricultural Education Division is planning its explorations in the following
areas.
A_results:
1. Establishing criteria for the selection of superior prospective teachers.
2. Analysis of the duties and responsibilities of the teacher of agriculture as
evidence for designing the program of teacher preparation.
3. Establish the relationship of the psychological and sociological foundations
to other phases of the teachers' professional preparation.
4. Design of the professional training program with emphasis on: Course sequences
and content, tutorial program, summer experience program, methods and techniques,experiences in student teaching centers, and criteria for the selection
of student teaching centers.
5. Educational program to qualify supervising teachers.
6. Placement and follow-up procedures to use with first year teachers.
In the academic year of 1962 - 63 the program will be in operation. Cutstanding students will be admitted at the fifth year level, if they have completed
a degree in agriculture. Outstanding senior students in the college of agriculture may be admitted to complete their preparation as teachers of agriculture.
Stipends will be available for students accepted in the progren.
